---
title: "Big Mac Index"
author: "Jim Gruman"
date: "December 31, 2020"
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: no
    code_folding: hide
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

> Derived from [Cedric Scherer's excellent post](https://github.com/Z3tt/TidyTuesday/tree/master/plots/2020_52) which in turn was inspired by [Robert Kosara](https://twitter.com/eagereyes)'s [eagereyes YouTube video](https://t.co/Nm6MdcBOyp?amp=1)

Our goal here is to explore *The Economist's* data behind their recurring [Big Mac Index story on global currencies](https://www.economist.com/news/2020/07/15/the-big-mac-index).

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://infographics.economist.com/2018/big-mac/index.html" data-size="full-width" ></iframe>

```{r setup}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	echo = TRUE,
	fig.height = 7.402,
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE,
	cache = FALSE,
	cache.lazy = FALSE,
	df_print = "paged",
	dpi = 300,
	tidy = "styler"
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(hrbrthemes)
extrafont::loadfonts(quiet = TRUE)

theme_set(hrbrthemes::theme_modern_rc() +
            theme(plot.title.position = "plot", 
                  plot.caption.position = "plot"))

library(gganimate)
library(here)
```

We will load the latest data directly from the #TidyTuesday github repo.

```{r load_data}
source_page  <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load("2020-12-22")

df_mac_raw <- source_page$`big-mac`
```

```{r prep-data}
df_mac <- 
  df_mac_raw %>% 
  mutate(year = year(date)) %>% 
  select(date, year, iso_a3, currency_code, name, dollar_price) %>% 
  group_by(iso_a3, name, year) %>% 
  summarize(price = mean(dollar_price)) %>% 
  group_by(iso_a3) %>% 
  filter(n() == 21)  # use only countries with complete data sets

highlights <- c("EUZ", "CHE", "CAN", "MEX", "BRA", "ARG", "GBR", "USA")
n <- length(highlights)
```

## Animation

```{r prep-index-plots-animation}
index_it <- function(y) {
  countries <- 
    df_mac %>% 
    filter(year == y) %>% 
    pull(iso_a3)
  
df_mac_indexed <- 
    df_mac %>% 
    group_by(iso_a3) %>%
    filter(iso_a3 %in% countries) %>% 
    mutate(
      ref_year = y,
      price_index = price[which(year == y)],
      price_rel = price - price_index,
      group = if_else(iso_a3 %in% highlights, iso_a3, "other"),
      group = as.factor(group)
    ) %>% 
    mutate(
      group = fct_relevel(group, "other", after = Inf),
      name_lab = if_else(year == 2020, name, NA_character_)
    ) %>% 
    ungroup()
}

df_mac_index_refs <- map_df(2000:2019, ~ index_it(.x))
```

```{r plot-and-animation, warnings = FALSE}
index_plots <- 
  ggplot(df_mac_index_refs %>% filter(group != "other"), 
         aes(year, price_rel, group = iso_a3), show.legend = FALSE) + 
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = seq(2000, 2020, by = 5),
    color = "grey91", 
    size = .6
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = tibble(y = seq(-6, 6, by = 2), x1 = 2000, x2 = 2020),
    aes(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "grey91",
    size = .6
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = tibble(y = 0, x1 = 2000, x2 = 2020),
    aes(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "grey40",
    size = .5
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    aes(yintercept = 0), 
    color = "grey90",
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) + 
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = ref_year), 
    color = "grey40",
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = df_mac_index_refs %>% filter(group == "other"),
    color = "grey70",
    size = .6,
    alpha = .5
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = group),
    size = .9
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = ref_year, y = 5.2, 
        label = as.character(ref_year)),
    stat = "unique",
    size = 8,
    color = "grey60",
    hjust = 0,
    nudge_x = .3
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(color = group,
        label = name_lab),
    fontface = "bold",
    max.overlaps = 20,
    size = 6.7,
    direction = "y",
    xlim = c(2020.8, NA),
    hjust = 0,
    segment.size = .7,
    segment.alpha = .5,
    segment.linetype = "dotted",
    box.padding = .4,
    segment.curvature = -0.1,
    segment.ncp = 3,
    segment.angle = 20
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    clip = "off",
    xlim = c(2000, 2024), 
    ylim = c(-6, 6)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = seq(2000, 2020, by = 5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = seq(-6, 6, by = 2),
    labels = scales::label_dollar(accuracy = 2, negative_parens = TRUE)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(rcartocolor::carto_pal(n = n, name = "Vivid")[1:n - 1], "grey50")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Compared to {closest_state}, how much more or less do you have to pay for a Big Mac today?", x = NULL, y = NULL,
    subtitle = "The animated series of <i>index charts</i> visualizes the price changes (in USD) of a Big Mac based on a selected index year. The <b>Big Mac Index</b> is published<br>by The Economist as an informal way to provide a test of the extent to which market exchange rates result in goods costing the same in<br>different countries. It <i>seeks to make exchange-rate theory a bit more digestible</i> and takes its name from the Big Mac, a hamburger sold at McDonald's restaurants.",
    caption = "The 27 countries that provide Big mac prices for all years from 2000 to 2020. For countries that reported twice per year, the mean value is shown.\nVisualization by @jim_gruman  •  Data by The Economist"
  ) +
  ## animation settings
  transition_states(ref_year, 5, 25, wrap = FALSE) +
  ease_aes('quadratic-in-out') +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
  axis.text = element_text(color = "grey40"),
  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 20, margin = margin(t = 5)),
  axis.text.y = element_text(size = 17, margin = margin(r = 5)),
  axis.ticks = element_line(color = "grey91", size = .5),
  axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.3, "lines"),
  axis.ticks.length.y = unit(.7, "lines"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = margin(20, 40, 20, 40),
  plot.title = element_text(color = "grey95", size = 32, face = "bold",
                            margin = margin(t = 15)),
  plot.subtitle = element_markdown(color = "grey60", size = 17, 
                                   lineheight = 1.35,
                                   margin = margin(t = 15, b = 40, r = 15)),
  plot.caption = element_text(color = "grey60", size = 15,
                              lineheight = 1.2, hjust = 0, 
                              margin = margin(t = 40)),
  legend.position = "none"
)

index_anim <- animate(index_plots, nframes = 100, 
          fps = 5, detail = 5, end_pause = 25,
          width = 1150, height = 740, 
          device = "png", type = "cairo", 
          renderer = gifski_renderer())

anim_save(filename = "BigMacIndex.gif", 
          path = here::here("docs", "assets"))
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("assets/BigMacIndex.gif", error = FALSE)
```
